summary: Plan is well-scoped: creates a single pure-function module with no side effects and no modifications to existing files. All upstream interfaces (BrowserSignals, GeoHint, PointerState) exist and match the plan's assumptions. The additive-only nature and pure-function design make this very low risk.

details: BrowserSignals exports: language, timezone, screenWidth, screenHeight, devicePixelRatio, hardwareConcurrency, prefersColorScheme, prefersReducedMotion, touchCapable — plan references timezone, devicePixelRatio, hardwareConcurrency, prefersReducedMotion correctly
GeoHint exports: country (string|null), region (string|null) — matches plan step 3
PointerState exports: x, y, dx, dy, speed, active — plan step 7 correctly uses speed and active fields
sessionSeed exports: getSessionSeed() returning string — plan passes seed as string input, compatible

architectural_impact.dependency_direction: mappings.ts imports types from input/ and seed/ — no reverse dependencies created, correct layering
architectural_impact.public_api_surface: One function (mapSignalsToVisuals) and two interfaces (MappingInputs, VisualParams) — minimal and clean

risks.risk: Render loop is currently a stub that doesn't consume VisualParams
risks.mitigation: Plan explicitly scopes to mapping definition only — render loop integration is a future story. No dead-code risk since the module is self-contained and testable.

risks.risk: Plan step 4 uses timezone string but BrowserSignals.timezone is an IANA string (e.g. 'America/New_York'), not a numeric offset
risks.mitigation: The plan also takes timeOfDay as a fractional-hours number in MappingInputs, so the caller converts timezone to local hour. The timezone string in BrowserSignals is informational context, not the primary input. Implementation should rely on timeOfDay, not parse timezone.

risks.risk: Helper functions not exported individually — tests must exercise all 7 mappings through the single mapSignalsToVisuals entry point
risks.mitigation: Since all helpers are pure and deterministic, testing through the composed function is viable. The test plan (step 10) accounts for this with targeted input variations.

suggestions.suggestion: Consider exporting individual helper functions for granular testability
suggestions.rationale: Testing 7 mappings through one function requires many fixture permutations. Exported helpers would simplify test isolation without increasing coupling.

suggestions.suggestion: Clarify who computes timeOfDay from BrowserSignals.timezone — the caller or the mapping
suggestions.rationale: MappingInputs includes both timezone (from signals) and timeOfDay (fractional hours). Implementation should document that the caller computes timeOfDay so the mapping stays pure with no Date dependency.

test_pattern_alignment.details: Plan follows existing patterns: describe/it structure with US-008 prefix, vi.spyOn for storage API verification, module isolation with vi.resetModules, jsdom environment. Test categories cover range validation, determinism, null safety, and privacy compliance — consistent with sessionSeed.test.ts patterns.

definition_of_done_coverage.no_console_errors: Pure functions with no DOM access — no console error risk
definition_of_done_coverage.no_forbidden_storage: Test step 10 category 7 spies on localStorage/sessionStorage/cookie
definition_of_done_coverage.scene_works_without_autoplay: Mappings return 0 for silent audio — graceful fallback
definition_of_done_coverage.ui_still_works: No UI modifications in this plan

verdict: Plan is architecturally sound. Pure-function design with correct interface alignment, no existing file modifications, and comprehensive test coverage. Approve for implementation.
